name: Ray Drummond
abstract: Raymond Charles Drummond (November 23, 1946 – November 1, 2025) was an American jazz bassist and teacher. He also had an MBA from Stanford University, hence his linkage to the Stanford Jazz Workshop.

interviews.title: Ray Drummond interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Bobby McFerrin & Friends, plus The New York Giants
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: We Remember Erroll - A Tribute to Erroll Garner

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Leggio
about_quotes.evidence: when I was with Thad and Mel, the whole rhythm section, Mel, Harold Danko, Ray Drummond and we had a trumpet player, we did an album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Leggio interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: There’s the Roulette record... Live At Laren in Holland from seventy-nine with a New York band. Red Rodney, John Eckert, Jimmy Knepper, Sam Burtis, Ronnie Cuber, Bennie Aranov, Ray Drummond, and Billy Hart.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: We got a band we call Generations Band out there... It was Bulldog Drummond – Ray Drummond, the bass player. That was the band we used
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Mays
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve played with such a wide variety of bass players... from Ron Carter to Ron McClure, Red Mitchell to Ray Drummond
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Mays interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 5, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: With you and Lovano, James Williams, Abercrombie, Ray Drummond, and Adam Nussbaum. ... Yes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: At one time we had Ben Riley and Ray Drummond... in the group I worked with him [Chick Corea]
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
